questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the area of the triangle shown with base $6$ and height $4$.

figure.caption: Triangle base and height
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,40' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='120' x2='100' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='90' y='135' font-size='8'>base 6</text><text x='105' y='85' font-size='8'>height 4</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In what ratio should rice costing $20$ per kg be mixed with rice costing $30$ per kg so that the mixture costs $24$ per kg?

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the area of the circle of radius $7$ shown (use $\pi=3.1416$, two decimals).

figure.caption: Circle of radius 7

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: THERMOMETER : TEMPERATURE :: BAROMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : quantity it measures'.

**Working.** A thermometer measures temperature; a barometer measures atmospheric pressure.

**Answer.** pressure.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Using A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, the sum of the letter values of the word 'COAL' is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add positional values of each letter.

**Working.** C$=3$, O$=15$, A$=1$, L$=12$; sum $=31$.

**Answer.** $31$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which two basic shapes make up the house-shaped figure shown?

figure.caption: A house outline: a square with a triangular roof on top.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='35' y='65' width='70' height='60' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='25,65 70,25 115,65' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.12)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Decompose the outline into recognisable shapes.

**Working.** The body is a square and the roof is a triangle.

**Answer.** A square and a triangle.

questions.subject: Igneous Petrology
questions.stem: A plagioclase has An$_{20}$. Its series index (0=albite…5=anorthite, by 20% steps An0/10/30/50/70/90) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Albite(0–10), oligoclase(10–30), andesine(30–50), labradorite(50–70), bytownite(70–90), anorthite(90–100).

**Working.**
$$An_{20}\Rightarrow 1.$$

**Answer.** $1$

questions.subject: Mathematics & Geostatistics
questions.stem: The trapezoidal rule approximates the area under a curve using
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trapezoidal rule joins successive points by straight lines.

**Working.**
Straight-line segments.

**Answer.** Straight-line segments

questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: Foraminifera are widely used in petroleum biostratigraphy because they are
options: abundant calcareous microfossils with rapid evolution
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Forams are abundant, environmentally sensitive, fast-evolving microfossils.

**Working.**
Calcareous microfossils.

**Answer.** Calcareous microfossils

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: A rock joint has cohesion $20\,$kPa and friction angle $25^\circ$. Under normal stress $50\,$kPa, the shear strength is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $120^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $55^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=55^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $55^\circ$
